project.name: nudity-detector
project.description: A Python-based desktop application designed to detect nudity in images and videos using multiple AI detectors (NudeNet, HelloZ NSFW). Provides an automated solution for identifying explicit content, suitable for content moderation, safety filters, and compliance checks. Features a GTK4/Libadwaita GUI.

nodes.label: LICENSE
nodes.description: GNU General Public License v3 governing the project's distribution and modification terms.

nodes.label: README.md
nodes.description: Main project documentation covering installation, usage, features, and output formats for the Nudity Detector application.

nodes.label: docker-compose.yml
nodes.description: Docker Compose configuration that runs the helloz/nsfw AI detection server on port 6086 with 10 workers.

nodes.label: requirements.txt
nodes.description: Python package dependencies including nudenet, openpyxl, Pillow, opencv-python, requests, Send2Trash, and darkdetect.

nodes.label: run_gui.py
nodes.description: Entry-point script that launches the GTK4 GUI application by calling main() from src/gui/app.py.

nodes.label: run_helloz_nsfw.py
nodes.description: Entry-point script that launches the Helloz NSFW CLI detector by calling main() from src/detectors/helloz_nsfw.py.

nodes.label: run_nudenet.py
nodes.description: Entry-point script that launches the NudeNet CLI detector by calling main() from src/detectors/nudenet.py.

nodes.label: HellozNSFWService
nodes.description: Docker-hosted AI service (helloz/nsfw) that exposes an HTTP API endpoint for NSFW image detection.

nodes.label: NudeNetDetector
nodes.description: Local nudity detection backend using the nudenet Python library for image and video frame analysis.

nodes.label: GUIApplication
nodes.description: GTK4 + libadwaita GUI application providing model selection, threshold control, progress tracking, and detected-media review.

nodes.label: .openspec.yaml
nodes.description: OpenSpec metadata file declaring the schema type and creation date for the modernize-ui-scan-workflow change specification.

nodes.label: design.md
nodes.description: Architectural design document detailing context, goals, decisions, and risks for the modernized UI scan workflow change.

nodes.label: proposal.md
nodes.description: Change proposal describing what capabilities will be added or modified for the modernized UI scan workflow.

nodes.label: tasks.md
nodes.description: Task checklist tracking implementation progress across shared state, detection pipeline, GUI workflow, and documentation work.

nodes.label: ScanSessionPersistence
nodes.description: Design concept for persisting scan settings and detected results alongside reports to allow resuming moderation sessions.

nodes.label: DetectedMediaReview
nodes.description: UI concept for a structured results table showing detected explicit media with confidence scores and row-level actions.

nodes.label: ThemePreferences
nodes.description: Runtime theme selection capability supporting System, Light, and Dark modes in the GUI.

nodes.label: ConfigurableThreshold
nodes.description: Design decision to represent detection threshold as a percentage in the UI while normalizing to float for detector functions.

nodes.label: AppRun
nodes.description: AppImage entry point script that sets up GTK4, GLib, typelib, schema and theme environment variables before delegating to the PyInstaller binary.

nodes.label: build_linux.sh
nodes.description: Orchestrates the full Linux distributable build pipeline: venv setup, PyInstaller bundle, GTK4 typelib copying, AppDir assembly, linuxdeploy GTK plugin, and final AppImage creation.

nodes.label: nudity-detector.desktop
nodes.description: XDG desktop entry file that registers Nudity Detector as a GUI application in Linux application menus with icon and category metadata.

nodes.label: nudity-detector.spec
nodes.description: PyInstaller spec file that defines the one-dir bundle configuration, collecting all app modules, GTK/gi binaries, nudenet/vnudenet data, and hidden imports for the nudity-detector executable.

nodes.label: core.__init__
nodes.description: Empty package initializer that marks src/core as a Python package.

nodes.label: constants
nodes.description: Centralized constants module providing single source of truth for media types, nudity classes, thresholds, GUI config, threading, report headers, and API endpoint values.

nodes.label: models
nodes.description: Data model module defining type-safe dataclasses for scan configuration, report entries, session state, and raw detection results.

nodes.label: ScanConfig
nodes.description: Dataclass holding scan session configuration: source folder, model name, threshold percent, and theme mode.

nodes.label: ReportEntry
nodes.description: Dataclass representing a single detection result entry with file path, media type, confidence, nudity flag, classes, thumbnail, and date.

nodes.label: SessionState
nodes.description: Dataclass capturing the complete scan session state including version, save timestamp, ScanConfig, and list of ReportEntry results.

nodes.label: DetectionResult
nodes.description: Dataclass for raw detection output from a model, convertible to a ReportEntry via to_report_entry().

nodes.label: utils
nodes.description: High-level detection orchestration module providing thread-safe batch processing, file operations, report delegation, thumbnail generation, and the classify_files_in_folder worker-pool pipeline.

nodes.label: normalize_threshold
nodes.description: Normalizes a threshold value from percentage or decimal form to the [0, 1] range.

nodes.label: classify_files_in_folder
nodes.description: Walks a folder tree and dispatches image/video files to classifier callables using a configurable worker thread pool.

nodes.label: handle_results
nodes.description: Processes a detection result by generating a thumbnail, building a report entry dict, appending it to the global nudity_report, and periodically persisting to disk.

nodes.label: save_nudity_report
nodes.description: Saves detection results to an Excel report and serializes the session state JSON alongside it via ReportManager.

nodes.label: detect_with_timeout
nodes.description: Wraps a detector's detect() call in a daemon thread with an explicit timeout, raising TimeoutError if the detection stalls.

nodes.label: detectors.__init__
nodes.description: Empty package initializer that marks src/detectors as a Python package.

nodes.label: helloz_nsfw
nodes.description: CLI detector module that classifies images and videos for nudity by calling the Helloz NSFW HTTP API, using worker-pool batch processing and report persistence.

nodes.label: prompt_threshold_percent (helloz)
nodes.description: Prompts the user for a detection threshold percentage with validation and fallback to the default constant.

nodes.label: extract_frames (helloz)
nodes.description: Legacy wrapper around FrameExtractor to extract video frames using the CLI Helloz temp directory prefix.

nodes.label: main (helloz)
nodes.description: CLI entry point for the Helloz NSFW detector: loads existing report, prompts user for folder and threshold, runs batch classification, and saves results.

nodes.label: nudenet
nodes.description: CLI detector module that classifies images and videos for nudity using the NudeNet ONNX model, with worker-pool batch processing and report persistence.

nodes.label: simplify_nudenet_results
nodes.description: Converts raw NudeNet detection records to simplified dicts with class and score keys.

nodes.label: get_nudenet_confidence
nodes.description: Extracts the maximum confidence score across nudity-relevant class labels from a NudeNet detection result.

nodes.label: main (nudenet)
nodes.description: CLI entry point for the NudeNet detector: initializes NudeDetector, prompts user for folder and threshold, runs batch classification, and saves results.

nodes.label: gui.__init__
nodes.description: Empty package init file for the src/gui module.

nodes.label: app.py
nodes.description: Main GTK4/libadwaita application window file that wires all GUI mixin classes together.

nodes.label: NudityDetectorWindow
nodes.description: Primary Adw.ApplicationWindow subclass that composes all GUI mixins and builds the multi-tab UI.

nodes.label: __init__
nodes.description: Initialises window geometry, loads app config, sets all state fields, builds UI and loads the last session.

nodes.label: _build_ui
nodes.description: Constructs the toolbar view, header bar, and three-tab ViewStack (Scan, All Scans, Settings).

nodes.label: _build_settings_tab
nodes.description: Builds the Settings tab widget with controls for appearance, detection, processing, and Helloz NSFW configuration.

nodes.label: _build_scan_page
nodes.description: Builds the Scan tab widget including the scan settings grid, folder picker, and results area.

nodes.label: dialogs.py
nodes.description: Provides DialogsMixin with alert/error/warning/confirm dialog helpers using Adw.AlertDialog.

nodes.label: DialogsMixin
nodes.description: Mixin class offering _show_error, _show_warning, and _ask_yes_no convenience dialog methods.

nodes.label: preview.py
nodes.description: PreviewMixin handling thumbnail loading and preview panel updates for selected scan results.

nodes.label: PreviewMixin
nodes.description: Mixin that loads full-quality or fallback thumbnail images and renders them into a Gtk.Picture widget.

nodes.label: update_thumbnail_preview
nodes.description: Loads and displays the preview image for the currently selected result entry.

nodes.label: _load_preview_from_file
nodes.description: Loads a full-quality PIL image from the original image or video file for display.

nodes.label: _pil_to_pixbuf
nodes.description: Converts a PIL Image to a GdkPixbuf.Pixbuf via PNG encoding for GTK display.

nodes.label: result_item.py
nodes.description: Defines the ResultItem GObject model used as rows in the ColumnView results table.

nodes.label: ResultItem
nodes.description: GObject.Object subclass holding index, name, media_type, confidence, model_name, and path for a result row.

nodes.label: results.py
nodes.description: ResultsMixin for results table population, row selection, and row actions (open, open location, delete).

nodes.label: ResultsMixin
nodes.description: Mixin providing populate_results, append_results, row selection state, and file action handlers.

nodes.label: populate_results
nodes.description: Rebuilds the list store from scratch with the full set of detected results.

nodes.label: append_results
nodes.description: Incrementally appends only new result entries to the list store during an active scan.

nodes.label: delete_selected_result
nodes.description: Prompts for confirmation then deletes the selected file, removes it from report and list store.

nodes.label: scan_history.py
nodes.description: ScanHistoryMixin for the All Scans tab showing previous scan runs with load, export, and delete actions.

nodes.label: ScanRunItem
nodes.description: GObject model for a single row in the scan history ColumnView, holding date, model, result count, etc.

nodes.label: ScanHistoryMixin
nodes.description: Mixin building the All Scans tab and managing scan history list refresh, selection, and row actions.

nodes.label: refresh_scan_history
nodes.description: Re-reads the reports directory and rebuilds the history list store from dated scan subdirectories.

nodes.label: _export_from_session_json
nodes.description: Regenerates an xlsx report from a session JSON file when the original xlsx is absent.

nodes.label: scanning.py
nodes.description: ScanningMixin managing the full scan lifecycle including threading, classifier creation, and progress pulsing.

nodes.label: ScanningMixin
nodes.description: Mixin encapsulating scan start/stop, NudeNet and Helloz NSFW classifier factories, and worker thread orchestration.

nodes.label: start_scanning
nodes.description: Validates inputs, resets state, creates the initial scan run directory, and launches the processing thread.

nodes.label: process_files
nodes.description: Main worker-thread entry: counts files, creates classifiers, dispatches them via thread pool, and flushes intermediate results.

nodes.label: create_nudenet_classifiers
nodes.description: Creates image and video classifier closures that use NudeDetector with timeout handling.

nodes.label: create_helloz_nsfw_classifiers
nodes.description: Creates image and video classifier partials that POST to the Helloz NSFW HTTP API.

nodes.label: extract_video_frames
nodes.description: Extracts every Nth video frame to a temp directory using OpenCV for later classification.

nodes.label: session.py
nodes.description: SessionMixin handling session persistence, report file dialogs, and scan config helper methods.

nodes.label: SessionMixin
nodes.description: Mixin providing session save/load dialogs, auto-load on startup, and report open/browse actions.

nodes.label: load_session_from_path
nodes.description: Loads a session from an xlsx or JSON file, restores scan config fields and populates the results table.

nodes.label: load_initial_session
nodes.description: Auto-loads the most recent session on application startup.

nodes.label: app_config.json
nodes.description: User-specific runtime configuration file storing theme, model, threshold, worker, and Helloz NSFW connection settings.

nodes.label: ARCHITECTURE.md
nodes.description: Architecture overview document describing folder structure, module dependency graph, data flow, and build/distribution pipeline.

nodes.label: detected-media-review-spec.md
nodes.description: Specification requiring that detected explicit media be displayed with confidence and support open-location and delete actions.

nodes.label: scan-ui-settings-spec.md
nodes.description: Specification requiring configurable scan settings (folder, model, threshold) and persistent system/light/dark theme selection.

nodes.label: openspec config.yaml
nodes.description: OpenSpec schema configuration file for the spec-driven change management process.

nodes.description: Empty package init file for the src/processing module.

nodes.label: media_processor.py
nodes.description: Media processing utilities providing frame extraction, thumbnail generation, and media type detection.

nodes.label: detect_media_type
nodes.description: Detects whether a file is an image, video, or unknown type based on its extension.

nodes.label: is_supported_file
nodes.description: Returns True when detect_media_type identifies the file as a supported image or video.

nodes.label: FrameExtractor
nodes.description: Extracts every Nth frame from a video file into a temporary directory using OpenCV.

nodes.label: FrameExtractor.extract
nodes.description: Opens a video with OpenCV and writes sampled frames to a temp directory, returning (temp_dir, frame_paths).

nodes.label: FrameExtractor.cleanup
nodes.description: Removes the temporary frame directory created during extraction.

nodes.label: ThumbnailGenerator
nodes.description: Generates base64-encoded PNG thumbnails from image or video files using PIL and OpenCV.

nodes.label: generate_from_image
nodes.description: Opens an image with PIL, resizes it to thumbnail dimensions, and returns a base64-encoded PNG string.

nodes.label: generate_from_video
nodes.description: Extracts a representative frame from a video at a configured progress index and returns a base64 thumbnail.

nodes.label: ThumbnailGenerator.generate
nodes.description: Auto-detects media type and delegates to generate_from_image or generate_from_video.

nodes.label: reporting.__init__
nodes.description: Empty package init file for the src/reporting module.

nodes.label: report_manager.py
nodes.description: ReportManager class handling Excel report and JSON session file I/O including read, write, and embed-thumbnail logic.

nodes.label: ReportManager
nodes.description: Static-method class managing Excel report generation, session JSON persistence, thumbnail embedding, and directory validation.

nodes.label: ReportManager.load_entries
nodes.description: Reads an openpyxl workbook and returns a list of ReportEntry objects mapped from header columns.

nodes.label: ReportManager.save_entries
nodes.description: Writes ReportEntry objects to an Excel workbook, clearing previous data and embedding thumbnails.

nodes.label: ReportManager._embed_thumbnails
nodes.description: Decodes base64 thumbnails and embeds them as openpyxl images into the Thumbnail column of the worksheet.

nodes.label: ReportManager.save_session
nodes.description: Serialises a SessionState object to a JSON file alongside the report xlsx.

nodes.label: ReportManager.load_session
nodes.description: Loads a SessionState from a JSON file or falls back to an embedded sheet in the xlsx.

nodes.label: ReportManager.validate_report_dir
nodes.description: Validates that the report directory is writable and not a system-protected path.

The scan-ui-settings spec documents the scan configuration and theme selection requirements fulfilled in app.py.

tour.title: Guided Tour: nudity-detector
tour.description: A Python application for detecting nudity/NSFW content in images and videos. It supports two detection backends — a local NudeNet ONNX model and a Docker-hosted Helloz NSFW HTTP API — and exposes both a GTK4 GUI and CLI entry points. Walk this tour from project overview down through domain models, detection pipeline, and infrastructure.

stops.title: Project Overview
stops.narrative: Start here to understand the two detection backends (NudeNet local vs Helloz Docker API), supported media types, CLI vs GUI usage patterns, and output formats. The README also shows how to install dependencies and run each entry point.

stops.title: Core Constants
stops.narrative: All shared magic values live here: nudity class labels, default confidence thresholds, GUI config, threading settings, Excel report column headers, and the Helloz API endpoint. Changing behaviour project-wide almost always starts here.

stops.title: Domain Data Models
stops.narrative: Four typed dataclasses define the data contract for the whole app: ScanConfig (user inputs), DetectionResult (raw model output), ReportEntry (normalised result row), and SessionState (full scan snapshot for persistence). Understand these before reading any detector or GUI code.

stops.title: Detection Orchestration Pipeline
stops.narrative: This is the engine: classify_files_in_folder walks a folder tree and fans out to a configurable thread-pool of detector callables; handle_results normalises each hit into a ReportEntry and periodically flushes to disk; detect_with_timeout guards against stalled detector calls. All detector backends and the GUI thread both drive this pipeline.

stops.title: NudeNet CLI Entry Point
stops.narrative: The simplest way to run the app: calls main() in src/detectors/nudenet.py, which initialises the local NudeDetector ONNX model, prompts for a folder and threshold, and feeds files through the shared worker-pool pipeline. Good starting point for tracing a full detection flow end-to-end.

stops.title: NudeNet Detector Backend
stops.narrative: Wraps the nudenet Python library into the shared classify_files_in_folder contract. Key helpers: simplify_nudenet_results flattens raw bounding-box records, and get_nudenet_confidence extracts the highest score across nudity-relevant classes to decide whether the threshold is crossed.

stops.title: Helloz NSFW Detector Backend
stops.narrative: Alternative backend that POSTs images to the Docker-hosted Helloz HTTP API instead of running inference locally. Shares the same worker-pool pipeline as the NudeNet backend, making the two backends interchangeable. Requires the Docker service (see docker-compose.yml) to be running first.

stops.title: GTK4 GUI Application Window
stops.narrative: NudityDetectorWindow composes every GUI mixin (dialogs, preview, scan controls, results table, settings) into a three-tab Adw.ApplicationWindow. __init__ bootstraps config and restores the last session; _build_ui wires the toolbar and ViewStack. This is the integration point for all GUI-layer behaviour.

stops.title: GUI Entry Point
stops.narrative: One-liner that calls main() from src/gui/app.py to launch the GTK4 application. Used directly during development and also referenced by the PyInstaller spec and AppImage runner for distribution builds.

stops.title: Helloz Service Infrastructure
stops.narrative: Defines the helloz/nsfw Docker container that powers the remote detection backend: exposes port 6086 with 10 workers. Spin this up before using the Helloz detector CLI or enabling the Helloz model in the GUI. The API endpoint URL is kept in src/core/constants.py.
